page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6t.13xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 52 dedicated physical cores and 52 vCPUs, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at a base clock speed of 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with a single thread per core, ensuring predictable execution performance without hyper-threading overhead.

The instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of approximately 1.85 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local NVMe or SSD storage, requiring external block storage volumes for persistent data. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 16 Gbps. No dedicated GPU accelerators are present on this instance type.

Given its high physical core count and low memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance is designed for compute-intensive workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The dedicated CPU allocation provides consistent performance, making it qualitatively cost-effective for parallel processing tasks, batch computing, and high-throughput web serving. It is appropriate for workloads such as continuous integration and delivery (CI/CD) pipelines, compute-heavy microservices, and distributed simulation tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6t.13xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 52 dedicated physical cores and 52 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. With a single thread per core configuration, the instance delivers predictable compute performance. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM, providing approximately 1.85 GB of memory per core, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. The hardware profile makes it appropriate for compute-heavy workloads such as batch processing, continuous integration pipelines, distributed simulations, and high-throughput web serving where consistent CPU performance is prioritized over large memory footprints.
